content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 1, 1994

Roy L. Paul
11819 Rives Avenue
Downey, CA 90241

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-94-168

Dear Mr. Paul:

You have requested assistance concerning the campaign
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/
Your letter states only a general question. Therefore, we
consider your letter to be a request for informal assistance
pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

QUESTION

You are a candidate for Judge of the Downey Municipal Court,
Seat No. 3. Are you obligated to report volunteer services
provided to your campaign by a person who has, upon occasion,
acted as a paid advertising consultant, but is now a law student
and expects no compensation from the campaign?

CONCLUSION

The donation of volunteer personal services is expressly
excluded from the definition of "contribution" as set forth in the
Act. This applies regardless of the volunteer's occupation or
profession.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Roy L. Paul
Our File No. I-94-168
Page 2


ANALYSIS

Government Code Section 82015, which defines the term
"contribution," provides:

    ...Notwithstanding the foregoing definition of
"contribution," the term does not include volunteer personal
services or payments made by any individual for his or her
own travel expenses if such payments are made voluntarily
without any understanding or agreement that they shall be,
directly or indirectly, repaid to him or her.

If you have any questions concerning this advice, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Trish Mayer
                                        Political Reform Consultant
